title: mikania testudinaria
intro: Mikania testudinaria, also known as tortoise bindweed or tortoise creeper, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Mikania testudinaria include reducing pain and inflammation, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Mikania testudinaria is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being applied topically to the skin for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
